You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 87153ae1).

## Issue to Resolve

Formatting enum class with member definition and missing trailing commas causes rule violations on first run
## Expected Behavior
<!---Tell us what should happen. -->
<!-- If relevant, provide a link to the relevant section in the [Kotlin Coding Conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) or [Android Kotlin Style Guide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide).  -->

<!-- Note: Ktlint should not produce any code which conflict with IntelliJ default code formatting. So please verify that your expectation is accepted by IntelliJ default code formatting. -->

When formatting and validating the following enum class, the ktlint command should exit successfully (status `0`):

```kotlin
enum class TestEnum() {
    A,
    B;

    companion object {
        const val A_NUMBER = 1L
    }
}
```

## Observed Behavior
<!---Tell us what happens instead of the expected behavior -->
<!--- Provide the exact command which was executed but please -->
<!--- ensure that the flag "--log-level=debug" is added to the -->
<!--- command as well. Provide the output of this command. -->

When running ktlint on the above enum class, it is formatted correctly, but the run fails with rule violations (exit status `1`):
```bash
$ ktlint --format 
17:25:00.694 [main] INFO com.pinterest.ktlint.internal.KtlintCommandLine - Enable default patterns [**/*.kt, **/*.kts]
/home/karl/tasks/ktlint-issue-example/TestEnum.kt:1:1: Missing trailing comma before "}" (trailing-comma-on-declaration-site)
/home/karl/tasks/ktlint-issue-example/TestEnum.kt:5:4: Unnecessary semicolon (no-semi)

Summary error count (descending) by rule:
  no-semi: 1
  trailing-comma-on-declaration-site: 1
Exit status: 1 
```

I note that the line number for the `no-semi` violation is the line number after formatting.

A second run on the (now properly formatted) code runs correctly (exit status `0`):
```bash
$ ktlint --format
17:25:05.397 [main] INFO com.pinterest.ktlint.internal.KtlintCommandLine - Enable default patterns [**/*.kt, **/*.kts]
Exit status: 0
```

## Steps to Reproduce
Run command on above enum class:
```ktlint --format```

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.48.1
* Operating System and version: Arch Linux

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

I have the same issue

See release notes of 0.48.0:
> * The default value for trailing comma's on call site is changed to `true` unless the `android codestyle` is enabled. Note that KtLint from a consistency viewpoint *enforces* the trailing comma on call site while default IntelliJ IDEA formatting only *allows* the trailing comma but leaves it up to the developer's discretion. ([#1670](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1670))

@paul-dingemans I don't quite follow. My issue is that after doing formatting, ktlint reports invalid  `no-semi` and `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` rule violations. A subsequent run of ktlint (correctly) passes the same code.

Ah now I see. Although it is not visual in the textual representation of the code, the trailing comma is not inserted at the correct place in the AST representation of the code. I will try to fix it.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `RELEASING.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

152â†’    private fun visitTypeList(
153â†’        node: ASTNode,
154â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
155â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
156â†’    ) {
157â†’        val inspectNode = node
158â†’            .children()
159â†’            .first { it.elementType == ElementType.GT }
160â†’        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
161â†’            inspectNode = inspectNode,
162â†’            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
163â†’            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
164â†’            emit = emit,
165â†’        )
166â†’    }
167â†’
168â†’    private fun visitWhenEntry(
169â†’        node: ASTNode,
170â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
171â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
172â†’    ) {
173â†’        val psi = node.psi
174â†’        require(psi is KtWhenEntry)
175â†’        if (psi.isElse || psi.parent.cast<KtWhenExpression>().leftParenthesis == null) {
176â†’            // no commas for "else" or when there are no opening parenthesis for the when-expression
177â†’            return
178â†’        }
179â†’
180â†’        val inspectNode = node
181â†’            .children()
182â†’            .first { it.elementType == ARROW }
183â†’        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
184â†’            inspectNode = inspectNode,
185â†’            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
186â†’            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
187â†’            emit = emit,
188â†’        )
189â†’    }
190â†’    private fun visitClass(
191â†’        node: ASTNode,
192â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
193â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
194â†’    ) {
195â†’        val psi = node.psi
196â†’        require(psi is KtClass)
197â†’
198â†’        node
199â†’            .takeIf { psi.isEnum() }
200â†’            ?.findChildByType(ElementType.CLASS_BODY)
201â†’            ?.takeUnless {
202â†’                // Do nothing when last two entries are on same line as no trailing comma should be inserted
203â†’                it.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine()
204â†’            }?.let { classBody ->
205â†’                val nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition = classBody.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()
206â†’                node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
207â†’                    inspectNode = nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition,
208â†’                    isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
209â†’                    autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
210â†’                    emit = emit,
211â†’                )
212â†’            }
213â†’    }
214â†’
215â†’    /**
216â†’     * Determines the [ASTNode] before which the trailing comma is allowed.
217â†’     *
218â†’     * If the list of enumeration entries is terminated by a semicolon, that semicolon will be returned. Otherwise, the
219â†’     * last element of the class.
220â†’     */
221â†’    private fun ASTNode.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition(): ASTNode {
222â†’        val lastEnumEntry = children().last { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
223â†’
224â†’        val semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry = lastEnumEntry
225â†’            .children()
226â†’            .singleOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.SEMICOLON }
227â†’
228â†’        return semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry ?: lastChildNode
229â†’    }
230â†’
231â†’    private fun ASTNode.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine(): Boolean {
232â†’        val lastTwoEnumEntries =
233â†’            children()
234â†’                .filter { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
235â†’                .toList()
236â†’                .takeLast(2)
237â†’
238â†’        return lastTwoEnumEntries.count() == 2 && noNewLineInClosedRange(lastTwoEnumEntries[0], lastTwoEnumEntries[1])
239â†’    }
240â†’
241â†’    private fun ASTNode.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
242â†’        inspectNode: ASTNode,
243â†’        isTrailingCommaAllowed: Boolean,
244â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
245â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
246â†’    ) {
247â†’        val prevLeaf = inspectNode.prevLeaf()
248â†’        val trailingCommaNode = prevLeaf?.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull()
249â†’        val trailingCommaState = when {
250â†’            isMultiline(psi) -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.EXISTS else TrailingCommaState.MISSING
251â†’            else -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT else TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS
252â†’        }
253â†’        when (trailingCommaState) {
254â†’            TrailingCommaState.EXISTS ->
255â†’                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
256â†’                    inspectNode
257â†’                        .treeParent
258â†’                        .takeIf { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
259â†’                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
260â†’                        ?.prevLeaf()
261â†’                        ?.let { lastNodeBeforeArrow ->
262â†’                            if (lastNodeBeforeArrow.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !lastNodeBeforeArrow.textContains('\n')) {
263â†’                                emit(
264â†’                                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
265â†’                                    "Expected a newline between the trailing comma and  \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
266â†’                                    true,
267â†’                                )
268â†’                                if (autoCorrect) {
269â†’                                    val parentIndent = "\n" + inspectNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
270â†’                                    lastNodeBeforeArrow.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(parentIndent)
271â†’                                }
272â†’                            }
273â†’                        }
274â†’                } else {
275â†’                    emit(
276â†’                        trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
277â†’                        "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
278â†’                        true,
279â†’                    )
280â†’                    if (autoCorrect) {
281â†’                        this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
282â†’                    }
283â†’                }
284â†’            TrailingCommaState.MISSING ->
285â†’                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
286â†’                    val addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry = addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen()
287â†’                    val prevNode = inspectNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
288â†’                    if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
289â†’                        emit(
290â†’                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
291â†’                            "Missing trailing comma and newline before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
292â†’                            true,
293â†’                        )
294â†’                    } else {
295â†’                        emit(
296â†’                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
297â†’                            "Missing trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
298â†’                            true,
299â†’                        )
300â†’                    }
301â†’                    if (autoCorrect) {
302â†’                        if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
303â†’                            val parentIndent = "\n" + prevNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
304â†’                            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
305â†’                            if (leafBeforeArrow != null && leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace) {
306â†’                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
307â†’                                leafBeforeArrow.replace(newLine)
308â†’                            } else {
309â†’                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
310â†’                                prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(newLine, prevNode.psi)
311â†’                            }
312â†’                        }
313â†’
314â†’                        if (inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY) {
315â†’                            with(KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi)) {
316â†’                                val parentIndent =
317â†’                                    (prevNode.psi.parent.prevLeaf() as? PsiWhiteSpace)?.text
318â†’                                        ?: "\n${prevNode.lineIndent()}"
319â†’                                val newline = createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
320â†’                                val enumEntry = inspectNode.treeParent.psi
321â†’                                enumEntry.apply {
322â†’                                    add(newline)
323â†’                                    removeChild(inspectNode)
324â†’                                    parent.addAfter(createSemicolon(), this)
325â†’                                }
326â†’                            }
327â†’                        }
328â†’
329â†’                        val comma = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createComma()
330â†’                        prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)
331â†’                    }
332â†’                }
333â†’            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {
334â†’                emit(
335â†’                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
336â†’                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
337â†’                    true,
338â†’                )
339â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
340â†’                    this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
341â†’                }
342â†’            }
343â†’            TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS -> Unit
344â†’        }
345â†’    }
346â†’
347â†’    private fun ASTNode.getWhenEntryIndent() =
348â†’        // The when entry can be a simple value but might also be a complex expression.
349â†’        parents()
350â†’            .last { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
351â†’            .lineIndent()
352â†’
353â†’    private fun isMultiline(element: PsiElement): Boolean = when {
354â†’        element.parent is KtFunctionLiteral -> isMultiline(element.parent)
355â†’        element is KtFunctionLiteral -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.valueParameterList!!, element.arrow!!)
356â†’        element is KtWhenEntry -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.firstChild, element.arrow!!)
357â†’        element is KtDestructuringDeclaration -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.lPar!!, element.rPar!!)
358â†’        element is KtValueArgumentList && element.children.size == 1 && element.anyDescendantOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>() -> {
359â†’            // special handling for collection literal
360â†’            // @Annotation([
361â†’            //    "something",
362â†’            // ])
363â†’            val lastChild = element.collectDescendantsOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>().last()
364â†’            containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(lastChild.rightBracket!!, element.rightParenthesis!!)
365â†’        }
366â†’        element is KtParameterList && element.parameters.isEmpty() -> false
367â†’        else -> element.textContains('\n')
368â†’    }
369â†’
370â†’    private fun ASTNode.addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen() =
371â†’        if (psi is KtWhenEntry) {
372â†’            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
373â†’            !(leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace && leafBeforeArrow.textContains('\n'))
374â†’        } else {
375â†’            false
376â†’        }
377â†’
378â†’    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
379â†’        val codeLeaf = if (isCodeLeaf()) {
380â†’            this
381â†’        } else {
382â†’            prevCodeLeaf()
383â†’        }
384â†’        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }
385â†’    }
386â†’
387â†’    private fun containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(from: PsiElement, to: PsiElement): Boolean {
388â†’        var leaf: PsiElement? = from
389â†’        while (leaf != null && !leaf.isEquivalentTo(to)) {
390â†’            if (leaf.textContains('\n')) {
391â†’                return true
392â†’            }
393â†’            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf(skipEmptyElements = false)
394â†’        }
395â†’        return leaf?.textContains('\n') ?: false
396â†’    }
397â†’
398â†’    private enum class TrailingCommaState {
399â†’        /**
400â†’         * The trailing comma is needed and exists
401â†’         */
402â†’        EXISTS,
403â†’
404â†’        /**
405â†’         * The trailing comma is needed and doesn't exist
406â†’         */
407â†’        MISSING,
408â†’
409â†’        /**
410â†’         * The trailing comma isn't needed and doesn't exist
411â†’         */
412â†’        NOT_EXISTS,
413â†’
414â†’        /**
415â†’         * The trailing comma isn't needed, but exists
416â†’         */
417â†’        REDUNDANT,
418â†’    }
419â†’
420â†’    public companion object {
421â†’        private val BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET = setOf("true", "false")
422â†’
423â†’        public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
424â†’            EditorConfigProperty(
425â†’                type = PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
426â†’                    "ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma",
427â†’                    "Defines whether a trailing comma (or no trailing comma) should be enforced on the defining " +
428â†’                        "side, e.g. parameter-list, type-argument-list, lambda-value-parameters, enum-entries, etc." +
429â†’                        "When set, IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to allow usage of a trailing comma by discretion " +
430â†’                        "of the developer. KtLint however uses this setting to enforce consistent usage of the " +
431â†’                        "trailing comma when set.",
432â†’                    PropertyValueParser.BOOLEAN_VALUE_PARSER,
433â†’                    BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET,
434â†’                ),
435â†’                defaultValue = true,
436â†’                defaultAndroidValue = false,
437â†’            )
438â†’
439â†’        @Deprecated(
440â†’            message = "Marked for removal in KtLint 0.49",
441â†’            replaceWith = ReplaceWith("TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY"),
442â†’        )
443â†’        @Suppress("ktlint:experimental:property-naming")
444â†’        public val allowTrailingCommaProperty: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
445â†’            TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY
446â†’
447â†’        private val TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE = TokenSet.create(
448â†’            CLASS,
449â†’            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION,
450â†’            FUNCTION_LITERAL,
451â†’            FUNCTION_TYPE,
452â†’            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST,
453â†’            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST,
454â†’            WHEN_ENTRY,
455â†’        )
456â†’    }
457â†’}

 56â†’        visitorModifiers = setOf(

 58â†’                ruleId = "standard:wrapping",
 59â†’                loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded = true,
 60â†’                runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled = true,

 81â†’        node: ASTNode,
 82â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 83â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84â†’    ) {
 85â†’        // Keep processing of element types in sync with Intellij Kotlin formatting settings.
 86â†’        // https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-kotlin/blob/master/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/formatter/trailingComma/util.kt
 87â†’        when (node.elementType) {
 88â†’            CLASS -> visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 89â†’            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION -> visitDestructuringDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)

 91â†’            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 92â†’            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 93â†’            WHEN_ENTRY -> visitWhenEntry(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 94â†’            else -> Unit
 95â†’        }
 96â†’    }

353â†’    private fun isMultiline(element: PsiElement): Boolean = when {
354â†’        element.parent is KtFunctionLiteral -> isMultiline(element.parent)
355â†’        element is KtFunctionLiteral -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.valueParameterList!!, element.arrow!!)
356â†’        element is KtWhenEntry -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.firstChild, element.arrow!!)
357â†’        element is KtDestructuringDeclaration -> containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.lPar!!, element.rPar!!)
358â†’        element is KtValueArgumentList && element.children.size == 1 && element.anyDescendantOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>() -> {
359â†’            // special handling for collection literal
360â†’            // @Annotation([
361â†’            //    "something",
362â†’            // ])
363â†’            val lastChild = element.collectDescendantsOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>().last()
364â†’            containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(lastChild.rightBracket!!, element.rightParenthesis!!)
365â†’        }
366â†’        element is KtParameterList && element.parameters.isEmpty() -> false
367â†’        else -> element.textContains('\n')
368â†’    }

373â†’            !(leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace && leafBeforeArrow.textContains('\n'))

378â†’    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
379â†’        val codeLeaf = if (isCodeLeaf()) {
380â†’            this
381â†’        } else {
382â†’            prevCodeLeaf()
383â†’        }
384â†’        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }

387â†’    private fun containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(from: PsiElement, to: PsiElement): Boolean {
388â†’        var leaf: PsiElement? = from
389â†’        while (leaf != null && !leaf.isEquivalentTo(to)) {
390â†’            if (leaf.textContains('\n')) {
391â†’                return true
392â†’            }
393â†’            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf(skipEmptyElements = false)
394â†’        }
395â†’        return leaf?.textContains('\n') ?: false
396â†’    }
